channel.title: Perils of Technology on Robert Marshall
channel.description: Recent content in Perils of Technology on Robert Marshall

item.title: Dr. Strangelove and the Unchecked Perils of Technology
item.description: Stanley Kubrick’s Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb (1964) remains one of the most insightful cinematic critiques of technological escalation, political hubris, and the illusion of control. Although the film satirises Cold War-era nuclear tensions, its themes extend beyond the nuclear arms race to modern existential threats—most notably, artificial intelligence. The same factors that make nuclear war a perilous and uncontrollable force—unchecked technological development, human fallibility, and the breakdown of decision-making systems—are now mirrored in the rapid advancement of AI. As we move deeper into an age of intelligent machines, Dr. Strangelove serves as a cautionary tale for how blind faith in technology, combined with human misjudgement, can lead to irreversible consequences.
